title: Update quesadilla image and add creative header phrase

requirements.summary: Replace the quesadilla menu card image with a newly generated image showing a lightly cooked cheese-only quesadilla with no steam, plated with side portions of cilantro, onions, salsa, and sour cream
acceptanceCriteria: The quesadilla image shows no steam effects
The quesadilla appears lightly cooked / pale golden rather than deeply browned
Only cheese is visible as a filling inside the quesadilla
Cilantro, sliced onions, salsa, and sour cream are visible as side items in the image
The updated image is rendered in the quesadilla menu card

file_operations.description: Update the quesadilla menu card's image source to reference the newly generated image at frontend/public/assets/generated/quesadilla-cheese-sides.dim_800x600.png. Locate the quesadilla card (the fourth menu item in the grid) and replace its current image path with the new asset path. Ensure the image is properly loaded and displayed in the card's image container without affecting other menu items (tacos, burritos, chips & salsa).

requirements.summary: Add a short, creative, catchy Mexican food phrase or slogan inside the site header to make it feel less bland, styled to complement the existing header design
acceptanceCriteria: A creative Mexican food-themed phrase or slogan is visible in the header
The phrase is styled consistently with the existing header color palette and typography
Header navigation links and phone button remain fully functional and unobstructed
The phrase is legible on both desktop and mobile layouts

file_operations.description: Add a creative, catchy Mexican food phrase or slogan to the header component. Position the phrase between the logo and navigation elements, or integrate it as a tagline near the logo. Use a creative phrase such as 'Bold Flavors, Zero Regrets' or 'Where Tradition Meets Your Taste Buds'. Style the phrase using the existing Mexican-palette color tokens (chili, gold, verde) from the Tailwind config, ensure it complements the header's typography and layout, and verify it remains legible and properly positioned on both desktop and mobile viewports without obstructing navigation links or the phone button. Ensure the phrase is hidden or repositioned appropriately in the mobile hamburger drawer if needed.
